items.headline: Draymond Green, Devin Booker ejected in final minute of Suns' blowout win
items.blurb: Draymond Green, Devin Booker ejected in final minute of Suns' blowout win — Draymond Green made sure to go out with a bang on Friday as the Golden State Warriors’ season came to an end at the hands of Devin Booker and the Phoenix Suns.

items.headline: Warriors' Steve Kerr answers critics with play-in upset over Clippers
items.blurb: Warriors' Steve Kerr answers critics with play-in upset over Clippers — Steve Kerr has won four championships with the Golden State Warriors and reached the NBA Finals six times as a head coach. Yet he's faced a slew of criticism of his coaching this season as the injured Warriors slumped to a 37-win season.

items.headline: NBA stars under the most pressure to perform in playoffs
items.blurb: NBA stars under the most pressure to perform in playoffs — It's often said that the true legends step up their game when it matters the most. So, with the NBA playoffs here, the teams able to advance will often rely on their top players to take their game to another level.

items.blurb: Rockets announce concerning injury update on Kevin Durant — The Houston Rockets may be down a superstar of their own to open their first-round series against the Los Angeles Lakers.

items.headline: Defense wins play-in games: Magic shut down Hornets offense to earn final playoff spot in East
items.blurb: Defense wins play-in games: Magic shut down Hornets offense to earn final playoff spot in East — Since the start of 2026, the Charlotte Hornets have had the best offense in the NBA. Against the Orlando Magic, that offense looked like the league's worst.

items.headline: Paolo Banchero earns redemption in Magic's win over Hornets
items.blurb: Paolo Banchero earns redemption in Magic's win over Hornets — In earning some redemption and continuing to make a name for himself, Paolo Banchero put on a show on Friday night. Banchero's performance led the Orlando Magic to a 121-90 win over the Charlotte Hornets, securing an actual spot in the NBA playoffs.

items.headline: Aliyah Boston, Fever Sign Three-Year, $6.3M Extension
items.blurb: Aliyah Boston, Fever Sign Three-Year, $6.3M Extension — Aliyah Boston has agreed to a four-year, $6.3 million contract extension with the Indiana Fever. The contract is the largest contract in terms of total salary in WNBA history. Boston will earn $1 million in 2026, which is lower than the standard $1.19 million max deal she was eligible to sign. items.headline: Ty Lue To Remain Head Coach Of Clippers 'For A Long, Long Time'
items.blurb: Ty Lue To Remain Head Coach Of Clippers 'For A Long, Long Time' — Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank stated Friday that the franchise never considered replacing Tyronn Lue as head coach, even during their 6-21 start to the 2025-26 season. items.headline: Oscar Schmidt Passes Away At Age 68
items.blurb: Oscar Schmidt Passes Away At Age 68 — Basketball Hall of Famer Oscar Schmidt has passed away at the age of 68 due to a long battle with a brain tumor. Schmidt played in a record-tying five Olympics and four World Cups for Brazil and became the all-time leading scorer in both tournaments. items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Questionable For Game 1 With 'Right Knee Injury Maintenance' — Anthony Edwards has bene listed as questionable by the Minnesota Timberwolves for Game 1 of their first round playoff series agains the Denver Nuggets due to "right knee injury maintenance." items.headline: Clippers Signal They Want To Keep Kawhi Leonard
items.blurb: Clippers Signal They Want To Keep Kawhi Leonard — Los Angeles Clippers president of basketball operations Lawrence Frank publicly committed to building around Kawhi Leonard on Friday, days after the 34-year-old forward deflected questions about his own future following the team's play-in elimination. items.headline: Kevin Durant Questionable For Game 1 With Knee Contusion
items.blurb: Kevin Durant Questionable For Game 1 With Knee Contusion — Kevin Durant sustained a right knee contusion during practice this week and has been added to the Houston Rockets' injury report ahead of Game 1 on Saturday at the Los Angeles Lakers. The Rockets are optimistic that Durant's knee injury will not be a significant issue during the series and is listed as questionable for Game 1. The Rockets

items.blurb: Magic’s Banchero throws down dunk with authority over Hornets’ Diabate — Watch as Orlando Magic’s Paolo Banchero gets up high and throws down a dunk with authority over Charlotte Hornets’ Moussa Diabate, to extend his team’s lead in the third quarter of their play-in game.

items.headline: Five-time NBA All-Star issues upset alert for Nuggets-Timberwolves series
items.blurb: Five-time NBA All-Star issues upset alert for Nuggets-Timberwolves series — The first-round matchup between the Denver Nuggets and Minnesota Timberwolves carries more weight than most NBA playoff openers. Both teams entered the season expecting a legitimate title run, and their recent history adds another layer to the intrigue.

items.headline: Oscar Schmidt, Basketball Hall of Famer from Brazil, dies at 68
items.blurb: Oscar Schmidt, Basketball Hall of Famer from Brazil, dies at 68 — Oscar Schmidt, the Basketball Hall of Famer whom his Brazil compatriots know as the “Holy Hand,” died on Friday. He was 68.

items.headline: Raptors hope return to competitive relevance leads to long-term success
items.blurb: Raptors hope return to competitive relevance leads to long-term success — The Raptors have returned to competitive relevance by getting back into the NBA Playoffs as the No. 5 seed, but they must now prove that this is the start of something meaningful.

items.blurb: LeBron James Has Made No Decision On Future, Retirement Remains Genuine Option — LeBron James has not decided whether to continue his NBA career for the 2026-27 season, and retirement remains a genuine possibility, team and league sources granted anonymity to speak openly told The Athletic. items.blurb: How do East stars Barnes and Mobley matchup heading into Round 1? — NBA writer Justin Rowan joins The Raptors Show and discusses the star matchup of the Toronto Raptors’ Scottie Barnes and Cleveland Cavaliers’ Evan Mobley in the opening round and how important that duel is going to be over the course of the series.

items.headline: Draymond Green: Ejected in season-ending loss
items.blurb: Draymond Green: Ejected in season-ending loss — Green was ejected with 1:04 remaining in the fourth quarter of Friday's 111-96 Play-In Tournament loss to the Suns after fouling out at the 1:06 mark. He closed with five points (2-4 FG, 0-2 3Pt, 1-2 FT), six assists, two rebounds and one steal across 36 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Devin Booker: Tossed Friday
items.blurb: Devin Booker: Tossed Friday — Booker was ejected with 1:04 remaining in the fourth quarter of Friday's 111-96 Play-In Tournament win over the Warriors. He finished with 20 points (5-12 FG, 0-4 3Pt, 10-10 FT), eight assists, six rebounds and one steal across 42 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: LeBron James Could Consider Lakers, Warriors, Cavaliers, Clippers In Free Agency
items.blurb: LeBron James Could Consider Lakers, Warriors, Cavaliers, Clippers In Free Agency — LeBron James has acknowledged that he's nearing the end of his career as a basketball player. What that end will come is still unknown. Sources close to James said that he's still undecided about playing beyond this season or not. items.headline: Paolo Banchero: Scores game-high 25 points in win
items.blurb: Paolo Banchero: Scores game-high 25 points in win — Banchero registered 25 points (9-17 FG, 2-6 3Pt, 5-8 FT), five rebounds, six assists, two steals and one block over 33 minutes during Friday's 121-90 Play-In Tournament win over the Hornets. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: LaMelo Ball: Paces Charlotte in blowout loss
items.blurb: LaMelo Ball: Paces Charlotte in blowout loss — Ball contributed 23 points (7-17 FG, 3-8 3Pt, 6-6 FT), five assists and two rebounds across 25 minutes in Friday's 121-90 Play-In Tournament loss to the Magic. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Grayson Allen: Available Friday
items.blurb: Grayson Allen: Available Friday — Allen (hamstring) is available for Friday's Play-In Tournament game against the Warriors. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
